web_skills: **PRIORITY**: Check source code leaks FIRST: /www.zip, /www.tar.gz, /web.zip, /source.zip, /backup.zip, /app.zip, /dist.zip, /code.zip. Source code is the #1 key to Web CTF.
Look for HTML comments containing hints (<!-- -->)
Identify the framework from response headers (X-Powered-By) and page content, then try framework-specific routes
For Laminas/Zend Framework: try /album, /application, /user, /module. Check for custom actions like /album/imgupload, /album/imgdelete

For PHP apps with unlink()/file_exists()/is_file() on user input: this is a phar:// deserialization trigger. Build POP chain, generate phar, gzip compress, rename to .jpg, upload, trigger via phar:// on the vulnerable endpoint
Laminas POP chain for RCE: Logger→Writer\Mail→PhpRenderer→TemplateMapResolver (setBody→system). Use eventsToMail for command.
Test SQL injection on all input parameters: ' OR 1=1--, UNION SELECT
Check for SSTI: {{7*7}}, ${7*7}, try Jinja2/Twig/Mako payloads
Pickle deserialization: use subprocess.check_output for RCE, avoid WAF keywords
File upload bypass: change Content-Type, use double extensions (.php.jpg), gzip+rename for phar
JWT attacks: try none algorithm, weak secret brute force
SSRF: try http://127.0.0.1, http://localhost, cloud metadata endpoints
When source code is available, analyze it for: unserialize() calls, file operations on user input, eval/system/exec calls, SQL query construction, template rendering with user data
crypto_skills: RSA small e (e=3): if m^e < n, just take e-th root of c
RSA close primes: use Fermat factorization when |p-q| is small
RSA Wiener attack: when d < n^(1/4)/3, use continued fractions
Common modulus attack: same n, different e values, use extended GCD
XOR: if key is short, use frequency analysis or known plaintext
Base64 → Hex → ASCII: try multi-layer decoding
Caesar/ROT13: try all 26 shifts, look for flag{} pattern
AES ECB: detect repeated blocks, use cut-and-paste attack
misc_skills: binwalk -e for embedded files in images
Check EXIF data with exiftool for hidden messages
PNG: check for modified IHDR (wrong dimensions), trailing data after IEND
ZIP pseudo-encryption: flip encryption flag in central directory
pcap: filter by protocol, follow TCP streams, extract files
Steganography: LSB in PNG (zsteg), DCT in JPEG (steghide)
Morse code: dots and dashes separated by spaces
pwn_skills: checksec first: check NX, PIE, Canary, RELRO
Buffer overflow: find offset with cyclic pattern, overwrite return address
Format string: %p to leak stack, %n to write, %hhn for byte-by-byte
ROP: pop rdi; ret for first arg, ret for stack alignment on x64
ret2libc: leak libc address via puts@plt, calculate system offset
reverse_skills: strings command first: look for flag patterns, URLs, passwords
Check for UPX packing: upx -d to unpack
XOR encryption: find key array, reverse the operation
ltrace/strace to see library/system calls at runtime
general_tips: Always check page source (HTML comments often contain hints)
Look at HTTP response headers for clues (Server, X-Powered-By)
If you find source code, analyze it carefully for vulnerabilities
For Python/Flask apps: look for pickle, eval, exec, os.system usage
WAF bypass: try encoding (URL, double URL, unicode), case mixing, comments
When stuck: re-read the challenge description for missed hints
